projects.title: Hydro-Risk Price Forecasting Model
projects.tagline: A regime-sensitive framework modelling NZ wholesale electricity price risk tied to hydro storage.

businessProblem.context: New Zealand's wholesale electricity price is closely tied to hydro storage levels, inflows, HVDC transfer flows, and climate conditions, but price risk under low-storage regimes is hard to see coming from raw storage figures alone.
goals: Build a regime-sensitive framework capturing how hydro storage, inflows, HVDC flows, and climate indicators interact to drive price risk.
Identify tail-risk amplification under low-storage conditions.
Generate procurement insights that are usable by a non-technical audience.
painPoints: Price risk behaves differently depending on the prevailing storage regime, not as a single linear relationship.
Relevant signals are spread across separate hydro, inflow, HVDC, and climate datasets.

approach.methodology: Built a regime-sensitive framework modelling NZ wholesale electricity price risk using hydro storage, inflows, HVDC flows, and climate indicators, identifying tail-risk amplification under low-storage conditions to generate procurement insights.

insights: Price risk amplifies in the tail under low-storage conditions rather than scaling linearly with storage levels.
A regime-based view (rather than a single model across all conditions) better captures how price risk actually behaves.
recommendations: Use the regime framework to flag periods where tail-risk conditions are building, ahead of price moves.
Feed procurement decisions from the regime signal rather than storage percentile alone.
projects.roi: Produced procurement-relevant risk insight grounded in the specific conditions (storage, inflow, HVDC, climate) that drive NZ wholesale price tail risk.

projects.title: Credit Risk Segmentation & Customer Clustering
projects.tagline: Segmenting 30,000+ credit users into behavioural risk groups to enable risk-based pricing.
projects.org: Massey University — Multivariate Analysis for Big Data

businessProblem.context: Lenders benefit from grouping customers by behavioural risk rather than relying on a single blended risk score, so pricing can reflect actual risk profile.
goals: Segment a large credit user base into distinct behavioural risk groups.
Validate the segmentation with a classification approach.
Enable risk-based pricing from the resulting groups.
painPoints: High-dimensional customer data is hard to segment meaningfully without dimensionality reduction.
Segmentation needs to be validated, not just visually plausible.

approach.methodology: Applied PCA and clustering to segment 30,000+ credit users into behavioural risk groups, then validated the segmentation with discriminant analysis.

insights: Discriminant analysis validated the behavioural clusters with 94% classification accuracy.
Distinct behavioural risk groups emerged that a single blended score would not surface.
recommendations: Use the validated segments as the basis for risk-based pricing tiers.
Re-run segmentation periodically as behavioural patterns shift.
projects.roi: 94% classification accuracy validating the behavioural segments, enabling risk-based pricing by group.

projects.title: Financial Hardship Modelling
projects.tagline: Quantifying how income, gambling harm, and financial literacy interact to drive financial hardship.

businessProblem.context: Policy decisions around financial hardship support are stronger when they're based on how income, gambling harm, and financial literacy interact, rather than treating each factor in isolation.
goals: Model the interactions between income, gambling harm, and financial literacy.
Quantify their combined impact on financial hardship.
Support policy recommendations with statistically robust findings.
painPoints: These factors are correlated with each other, so isolating their individual contribution requires careful model diagnostics.
Findings needed to hold up to robustness checks to be usable for policy.

approach.methodology: Modelled interactions between income, gambling harm, and financial literacy using robust OLS regression, applying diagnostics to check the stability of the findings before drawing policy conclusions.
dataSources: Survey data on income, gambling harm, and financial literacy

insights: Income, gambling harm, and financial literacy interact rather than acting independently on hardship outcomes.
Robust regression diagnostics confirmed the interaction effects held up beyond a simple linear read of the data.
recommendations: Target policy support at the specific combination of risk factors identified, not each factor independently.
Use the diagnostic-checked model as a baseline for further hardship research.
projects.roi: Produced statistically robust findings on hardship drivers to support policy recommendations.

projects.title: KPI & Governance Dashboard Reporting
projects.tagline: Consolidating fragmented multi-source data into standardised KPI dashboards across 80+ global agencies.

businessProblem.context: KPI, SLA, billing, and utilisation data for 7 Omnicom networks and 80+ global agencies was spread across separate systems (Replicon, Hive, Jira, Adobe Workfront), making reporting to senior leadership and clients a heavily manual process.
goals: Build standardised dashboards covering KPIs, SLA adherence, billing, and utilisation.
Automate the underlying data workflows to cut manual reporting effort.
Keep governance compliant through structured financial approval forms and scope trackers.
painPoints: Source data lived across four separate systems with no unified reporting layer.
Reporting was manual, which limited time available for actual analysis.
Quality breaches and delivery delays needed cross-team coordination to resolve.

approach.methodology: Built and maintained 50+ Power BI and Excel dashboards, automated SQL and Excel-based data workflows, and consolidated data from Replicon, Hive, Jira, and Adobe Workfront into structured reports for senior leadership and client-facing stakeholders. Also designed financial approval forms and scope trackers used as project contracts to keep governance compliant.

insights: Automating SQL and Excel-based workflows reduced manual reporting time by 70%.
Coordinating across Resource Management, Project Controllers, and Client Success teams resolved quality breaches and delivery delays more consistently.
recommendations: Keep the automated reporting workflow as the standard rather than reverting to manual consolidation.
Extend the same financial approval/scope-tracker governance model to new engagements as a default.
projects.roi: 70% reduction in manual reporting time, freeing team capacity for higher-value analysis across 7 networks and 80+ agencies.

projects.title: Recruitment Analytics Dashboard
projects.tagline: Uncovering hiring trends from 1,000+ survey responses to inform marketing and BD strategy.

businessProblem.context: Marketing and business development teams needed a clearer read on hiring trends and candidate engagement patterns than raw survey exports could provide.
goals: Analyse survey data from 1,000+ participants to surface hiring trends.
Build dashboards that make recruitment metrics usable for non-technical stakeholders.
Communicate findings clearly to a professional audience in real time.
painPoints: Survey data on its own didn't reveal actionable patterns without structured analysis.
Stakeholders needed insights presented live, not just as a static report.

approach.methodology: Analysed survey datasets from 1,000+ participants using SQL and Excel to uncover hiring trends and candidate engagement patterns, then built interactive dashboards to visualise the recruitment metrics.

insights: Interactive dashboards surfaced hiring trends and engagement patterns not visible in the raw survey exports.
Presenting findings live during LinkedIn virtual sessions made the insights more actionable for stakeholders in real time.
recommendations: Use the dashboard as a recurring input to marketing and business development planning, not a one-off analysis.
projects.roi: Enabled data-driven hiring-trend decisions for marketing and business development teams.
